# @andes/themes

<br />

The themes package provides you a set of utils to allow you theming andes components.

<br />

## How to use

<br />

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

npm install --save @andes/themes

```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx

import { AndesThemeProvider, useThemeContext } from '@andes/themes';

```

## Examples

```jsx
import { yourCustomTheme } from 'thePathToYourCustomTheme';
...
<AndesThemeProvider theme={yourCustomTheme}>
    <Button hierarchy="loud">Continue</Button>
</AndesThemeProvider>
```

#### Styles injected by your own

Our default setup includes style injection using [React style](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/style).
However, you are free to choose your preferred method for injecting styles by using the stylesOption parameter in the theme provider.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> A custom component (`CustomStyledSection` in the example) must be the last child of each `AndesThemeProvider`.
> 
> It must get the styles from the provider and render them on the page.
> 
> In the following example we use [frontend-style](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-style#readme) to do it.

```jsx
import { Style } from 'frontend-style';

const CustomStyledSection = () => {
  const { getThemeStyles } = useThemeContext();

  return (
    <Style renderChildrenInPlace={renderChildrenInPlace}>
      {getThemeStyles()}
    </Style>
  );
};
```

```jsx
import { yourCustomTheme } from 'thePathToYourCustomTheme';

// SSR styles
const stylesOptions = {externalInject: true}

// CSR styles
// const stylesOptions = {buildOn: 'CSR', externalInject: true}
...
<AndesThemeProvider theme={yourCustomTheme} stylesOptions={stylesOptions}>
    <Button hierarchy="loud">Continue</Button>
    <CustomStyledSection />
</AndesThemeProvider>
```

### Creating yourCustomTheme

If you don't specify the theme prop, the AndesThemeProvider will use the ML theme as the default theme.

A custom theme is an object that follow this [pattern](./meli-themes/ml.json)

Pass the theme object with those properties and you will be ready to start customizing your app.
NOTE: Also you can define the theme as an inline object.

### Nested ThemeProviders

The inner `AndesThemeProvider` will merge `yourCustomTheme` and `yourNestedCustomTheme` into one theme object.
This means that, the theme used in the inner `AndesThemeProvider` will extend `yourCustomTheme` but overriding the properties that they have in common.

```jsx
import { yourCustomTheme } from 'thePathToYourCustomTheme';
...
<AndesThemeProvider theme={yourCustomTheme}>
    <Button hierarchy="loud">Continue</Button>
    <AndesThemeProvider theme={yourNestedCustomTheme}>
        <Button hierarchy="loud">Another button</Button>
    </AndesThemeProvider>
</AndesThemeProvider>
```

### Nested ThemeProviders - Override parent context styles

The inner `AndesThemeProvider` will ignore the parent context theme.
It will behave as if there was no theme set before, that is, they are isolated.

```jsx
import { yourCustomTheme, yourNestedCustomTheme } from 'thePathToYourCustomTheme';
...
<AndesThemeProvider theme={yourCustomTheme}>
    <Button hierarchy="loud">Continue</Button>
    <AndesThemeProvider theme={yourNestedCustomTheme} override>
        <Button hierarchy="loud">Another button</Button>
    </AndesThemeProvider>
</AndesThemeProvider>
```

### Libs usage

> [!TIP]
> For libs that extend andes using something similar to the example below.

> [!NOTE]
> As this is just an example it could have weaknesses.
>
> We recommend you adapting it to a code that suit your needs.

### `LibContext` wrapper with `AndesThemeProvider`

#### `LibContext` component

```jsx
import { AndesThemeProvider, useThemeContext } from '@andes/themes';
import { yourCustomTheme } from 'thePathToYourCustomTheme';

const LibContext = ({ children }) => (
  <AndesThemeProvider id="yourLibIdentifier" theme={yourCustomTheme}>
    {children}
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

const useLibStyles = () => {
  const { getThemeStyles } = useThemeContext();
  return getThemeStyles;
};

export { LibContext, useLibStyles };
```

#### Usage

##### Button with styles applied

```jsx
import { LibContext } from '@yourLib/pkg';

const App = () => (
  <LibContext>
    <Button>I am an styled button</Button>
  </LibContext>
);

export default App;
```

> [!TIP]
> You can also use nested theme providers. Using several nested theme providers may slow down performance, so use them carefully.

### Internal components usage

#### How to add the theme provider to a component

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import classNames from 'classnames';
import { useThemeContext } from '@andes/themes';

const Component = ({ children }) => {
  ...
  const { getThemedComponentClasses, themeHash } = useThemeContext();
  const classes = classNames(
   ...otherClassNames
   getThemedComponentClasses?.('<componentName>') // Example: getThemedComponentClasses?.('button')
  );
  ...

  return (
    <div className={classes} {...themeHash}>
      {children}
    </div>
  );
}

export default Component;
```

## `AndesThemeProvider` API

| Property        | Description                                                                                                | Type            | Default     | Required |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | ----------- | -------- |
| `baseTheme`     | Base theme to be used by the provider. The theme that you provide will be merged with the this base theme. | `ML \| MP`      | `ML`        |          |
| `children`      | Children to be wrapped with the provider.                                                                  | `node`          |             | ✅        |
| `id`            | Id to be used as an identifier internally in the context to identify your theme.                           | `string`        | `undefined` |          |
| `override`      | Flag to override the parent theme in the context.                                                          | `boolean`       | `false`     |          |
| `stylesOptions` | Styles options used to generate the theme's styles.                                                        | `StylesOptions` | `{}`        |          |
| `theme`         | Theme object to be used by the context.                                                                    | `ThemeObject`   | `{}`        |          |

### ThemeObject


> [!NOTE]
> These are the main properties of the theme object, you can find the full tree supported by Andes in this [link](./meli-themes/ml.json)

```jsx
  {
    core: {} // Optional
    colorSchemes: {
      light: {}
    } // Optional
    components: // Optional
  }
```

### StylesOptions

```jsx
  {
    buildOn: 'CSR' | 'SSR', // Indicates if the styles should be created on SSR or CSR.
    externalInject: boolean, // Determines whether styles should be injected externally using the `getThemeStyles` function. If set to false, styles will be injected internally by the theme provider.
    global: boolean // Indicates if the styles should be created at the body selector.
  }
```

## Creating custom component variants

The Andes theme provider allows you to use the `variants` subentry in the `components` entry to create your our custom variants.

These variants can have specific styles that should be applied to the component when the `customVariant` prop (or it plus other props) is passed to the component.

> [!WARNING]
> The order of the variants is important, so make sure that the styles that should win are specified last.

### Examples

#### Using variants with propsMatcher as object

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      variants: [
        {
          id: 'my-variant-large', // This will be the unique identifier of your variant
          propsMatcher: { customVariant: 'xlarge', size: 'large' },
          styles: {
            fontSize: '28px',
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  }
};
```

#### Using variants with propsMatcher as callback

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    badge: {
      variants: [
        {
          id: 'my-variant-dashed-white', // This will be the unique identifier of your variant
          propsMatcher: (props) => props.customVariant === 'white' && props.size === 'large',
          styles: {
            border: '2px dashed white'
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  }
};
```

## Tokenized components

### Color

| Component           | Variant tokenized                         | Status |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------ |
| `Accordion`         | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `AmountField`       | Base                                      | Done ✅ |
| `Badge`             | Pill - Icon - Dot                         | Done ✅ |
| `BottomSheet`       | Modal - No modal                          | Done ✅ |
| `BoxSelector`       | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `Breadcrumb`        | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `Button`            | Simple - With icon - FAB - DropdownButton | Done ✅ |
| `Card`              | Simple - Extended                         | Done ✅ |
| `Checkbox`          | Simple - Highlight                        | Done ✅ |
| `CarouselSnapped`   | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `DatePicker`        | Standalone - InputDatePicker              | Done ✅ |
| `Dropdown`          | Simple - Standalone                       | Done ✅ |
| `ExpandableSection` | Simple - highlighted                      | Done ✅ |
| `EmptyState`        | With Illustration - With Icon             | Done ✅ |
| `FileUploader`      | Tag - Thumbnail                           | Done ✅ |
| `FeedbackScreens`   | Simple - Congrats                         | Done ✅ |
| `FloatingMenu`      | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `Helper`            | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `InlineEdit`        | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `InputStepper`      | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `List`              | Base - Chevron - Checkbox - Radio - Pick  | Done ✅ |
| `Message`           | Simple - With Thumbnail - With Actions    | Done ✅ |
| `MoneyAmount`       | Base - Combo - Discount                   | Done ✅ |
| `Pagination`        | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `ProgressIndicator` | Linear - Circular                         | Done ✅ |
| `RadioButton`       | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `Snackbar`          | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `SearchBox`         | Simple - Split                            | Done ✅ |
| `SegmentedControl`  | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `Slider`            | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `SnackBar`          | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `Switch`            | Simple                                    | Done ✅ |
| `TextField`         | Simple - Split - Password - CodeInput     | Done ✅ |
| `Tabs`              | -                                         | Done ✅ |

| `TechnicalError`    | Screen - Snackbar                         | Done ✅ |
